author: Dr. Sarah Patel (MICU resident) / Dr. Daniel Foster (MICU attending)
content:
MICU ADMISSION / TRANSFER-IN NOTE — 2/16/2027, 4:45 PM (Hospital Day 2)
Patient: Nguyen, Lan T (MRN-90215888)
Transferred from: General Medicine 5N
Reason for transfer: Worsening respiratory failure, hypotension despite fluids, presumed severe sepsis from biliary source. Severe acute pancreatitis.
Interpreter: Vietnamese language line.

INTERVAL HISTORY SINCE ADMISSION (~18 hours):
Ms. Nguyen continued to deteriorate clinically overnight on the medicine floor despite ongoing resuscitation.
- IVF: Received ~4.5 L LR overnight + 1L NS in ED.
- 6 AM: Tmax 102.4 F. WBC up to 19.8 from 17.3. Repeat lactate 4.1 (worsening from 3.2). BP transiently 86/48 requiring 500 cc bolus.
- 8 AM: SpO2 down to 88-90% on 4L NC; up-titrated to 6L NC; SpO2 92%. RR 26. Crackles now diffuse on auscultation, not just bases.
- 9 AM: AM CXR (see IMG002): worsening bilateral interstitial markings consistent with developing ARDS vs aggressive resuscitation; small bilateral pleural effusions. NO consolidation, NO focal infiltrate. **Incidental note: report flags a 2.5 cm right upper lobe nodule, well-circumscribed, not present on review of any prior imaging at this institution. Radiologist recommends follow-up with dedicated CT chest.**
- 10 AM: GI ERCP performed at bedside in the ICU corridor — stone extraction from CBD with sphincterotomy, brisk drainage of pus from biliary tract (samples sent for culture). Procedure tolerated; pancreatitis-related vasoplegia continued through procedure.
- 11 AM-2 PM: BP labile, requiring small-dose norepinephrine (started 0.04 mcg/kg/min, weaned to 0.02). Tmax 102.6 F. Repeat lactate 3.8.
- 2:30 PM: Decision made for ICU transfer for severe sepsis + worsening respiratory status. ID consult called for review of broad-spectrum coverage in setting of biliary pus.
- 3:45 PM: Transferred to MICU (bed 6). On 6L NC, on low-dose norepi.

CURRENT VITALS ON MICU ARRIVAL (4:30 PM):
BP 104/58 on norepi 0.02 mcg/kg/min, HR 110, RR 24, T 101.8 F, SpO2 92% on 6L NC.

PHYSICAL EXAM:
General: Ill-appearing, fatigued, mildly altered. Responds to voice in Vietnamese; oriented to son's presence but disoriented to date and place.
HEENT: Scleral icterus persists. Mucous membranes dry.
CV: Sinus tach 105-115, no murmur. JVP not elevated.
Lungs: Crackles diffusely bilateral, R > L, with reduced breath sounds at right base. Moderate increase in work of breathing.
Abdomen: Distended; mildly tender to deep palpation in epigastrium and RUQ; less acutely tender than yesterday post-ERCP. Surgical drain post-ERCP not in place. Soft otherwise.
Extremities: Cool peripheries; pulses 1+ but symmetric. No edema. No calf tenderness.
Skin: Mild jaundice.
Neuro: Alert intermittently, oriented to son. Following commands. No focal deficits.

LABS / IMAGING (today):
- CBC (AM): WBC 19.8 (up from 17.3), Hgb 9.4 (down from 10.2), Plt 290.
- BMP (AM): Na 134, K 4.1, Cl 100, CO2 19, BUN 52, Cr 2.1 (up from 1.8, AKI worsening), Glu 168.
- LFTs (AM): T-bili 4.1 (up), ALP 142, AST 124, ALT 158.
- Lipase 1124 (up).
- Lactate 3.8 (down trend from earlier 4.1).
- ABG on 6L NC: pH 7.32 / pCO2 28 / pO2 62 / HCO3 18 / SpO2 92% → metabolic acidosis with respiratory compensation, mild hypoxemia.
- CXR (this AM): see IMG002.
- Blood cultures (admission set): coagulase-negative staph from 1/2 bottles — preliminarily favored contaminant pending speciation; ID consulted.
- Biliary fluid culture (from ERCP): pending; gram-stain Gram-negative rods.
- Urinalysis: bland; no nitrites or leukocytes; no protein.

IMPRESSION:
Severe acute biliary pancreatitis with biliary sepsis (now status post emergent ERCP with stone extraction + sphincterotomy and pus drainage from biliary tract), complicated by ARDS (presumed, based on bilateral interstitial markings + hypoxemia + clinical setting; P/F ratio 124 mmHg meets ARDS Berlin criteria), septic shock requiring low-dose vasopressor support, and progression of AKI on CKD.

MICU PLAN:

#1 SEPSIS / SEPTIC SHOCK (biliary source)
- Continue pip/tazo 3.375 g IV q6h (renally adjusted upward given current Cr).
- Adding vancomycin pending blood culture clarification (CoNS — likely contaminant but high-acuity setting).
- Awaiting biliary fluid culture and sensitivities.
- Continue norepi titrated to MAP >65.
- Lactate q6h until clearance.
- Strict I/O. Foley placed for accurate UOP measurement.

#2 ARDS (Berlin criteria — mild-to-moderate)
- Currently on 6L NC, SpO2 92%, RR 24, ABG with P/F 124.
- Lung-protective strategy if intubation needed. If escalates beyond HFNC, will intubate.
- HFNC at bedside (initiating now at 40 L/min, FiO2 0.5).
- Daily CXR.
- Conservative fluid management going forward (now post-resuscitation phase).
- Will consider trial of awake proning if tolerated.

#3 ACUTE PANCREATITIS
- NPO continues. NGT placement reviewed; not necessary at this time absent obstruction.
- IV pain management (continue morphine PRN).
- Trend lipase (less useful prognostically); monitor clinical course.
- General Surgery aware; cholecystectomy deferred until acute illness resolves.

#4 AKI ON CKD (Cr 2.1 from baseline 1.3)
- Continue volume repletion, but transitioning to maintenance-level given third spacing concern.
- Hold lisinopril, hold ibuprofen.
- Renally dose all medications.
- Nephrology consult — will need to balance renal recovery vs ongoing IVF needs.
- Avoid contrast.

#5 RESPIRATORY FAILURE — see #2.

#6 BILIARY OBSTRUCTION — status post ERCP today with stone extraction and sphincterotomy; pus drained from biliary tract; cultures sent.

#7 ALTERED MENTAL STATUS
- Likely septic encephalopathy + hepatic involvement (mild) + sleep deprivation. Reassess as sepsis improves.

#8 DVT PROPHYLAXIS
- Mechanical (SCDs). Hold heparin SC pending platelets and bleeding risk re-evaluation post-ERCP.

#9 NUTRITION
- NPO. Nutrition consult tomorrow to advise on starting parenteral vs enteral nutrition planning.

#10 COMMUNICATION / FAMILY
- Vietnamese interpreter required for all updates with patient and family.
- Son Quang at bedside; updated re: ARDS and ICU course expectations. Family understands escalation risk.
- Code status: FULL CODE (re-confirmed).
- Healthcare proxy designation: verbally identifying son Quang; will work with social work tomorrow on formalization if able.

#11 OTHER
- LTBI documented; no symptoms of reactivation; will monitor.
- Insulin sliding scale for hyperglycemia in setting of stress + transient steroid (single-dose pre-med).
- Skin care: turn q2h, heel offloading, low-air-loss mattress.

In-person Vietnamese interpreter arrived at 4 PM and remained through admission discussion (1 hour). Will arrange daily interpreter rounds.
attestation:
Electronically signed by:
Dr. Sarah Patel, MD (MICU resident)
Dr. Daniel Foster, MD (MICU attending)
Date: 2/16/2027
